description:
Add create as a lifecycle event: Event enum variant + a dispatch point in the task-creation command path. Subscription is describe-gated exactly like claim/review/close/update; old plugins never declare it so are never invoked; additive per the prereq's rule. This removes the observe-death-but-not-birth asymmetry (mirror-on-create into Jira/Linear, etc.).

Resolve drop: SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants.md S6/S16 currently forbids drop participation by fiat ("nothing to negotiate"). The "agent walked away -> notify Jira" case is real. Make the decision explicit in the SPEC amendment: either (a) add drop as an OBSERVE-ONLY event — no negotiation, best-effort notification only, consistent with "drop changes no durable Task"; or (b) keep it forbidden with the rationale written down. Prefer (a) unless it forces a negotiation where there is no durable state. Record the decision and its why in the SPEC, not in a code comment.

WHY: "any state transition" was the bar. create and drop are the two transitions with zero extensibility today. create is unambiguously additive; drop needs an explicit, recorded decision rather than an inherited fiat.
title: create lifecycle event; resolve drop participation
